inlineContent.text: macOS virtual machines don’t have a cloud-init equivalent. Spooktacular
provides provisioning modes to bridge this gap. Today, SSH provisioning
is fully working. Disk-inject is in progress, and two more modes (guest
agent and shared-folder watcher) are planned.
After boot, Spooktacular discovers the VM’s IP, connects via SSH,
and executes your script. You get real-time output streaming.
Remote Login enabled in the guest
Before the VM boots, Spooktacular mounts the guest’s data volume
and writes a standard macOS LaunchDaemon that executes your script.
This is the only mode that will work on a completely vanilla macOS
install. This mode is currently being implemented.
The Spooktacular guest agent will communicate over VirtIO socket — a
direct host-guest channel that works without networking. This mode
is planned for a future release.
The script will be delivered via a VirtIO shared folder. A watcher
daemon in the guest will execute new scripts automatically. This
mode is planned for a future release.

abstract.text: Run scripts automatically when a VM boots.
Run Spooktacular on AWS EC2 Mac dedicated hosts to double your macOS CI capacity.
Set up a BlueBubbles iMessage server in a macOS VM — a dramatically simpler alternative to VMware on Windows.
Understand what version numbers mean and what to expect from each release.
Delivers the script via a VirtIO shared folder.
Run self-hosted macOS GitHub Actions runners in Spooktacular VMs with ephemeral isolation and automatic scaling.
Create and manage macOS virtual machines on Apple Silicon.
Executes the script over SSH after the VM boots.
Run ML training and inference on Apple Silicon GPUs inside Spooktacular virtual machines.
Create your first macOS virtual machine in minutes.
A virtual machine bundle stored on disk.
Connect to Spooktacular VMs via VNC for remote development, QA testing, and design review.
Injects a LaunchDaemon into the VM’s disk before first boot.
Manage macOS virtual machines from the terminal with the
Uses a pre-installed guest agent communicating over VirtIO socket.
The strategy for executing a user-data script on a VM.
The hardware specification for a virtual machine.
Manage macOS virtual machines as Kubernetes resources using the MacOSVM custom resource definition.
